column1:
BANNED WORDS (global): "seamless", "robust", "leverage", "empower",
"streamline", "comprehensive solution", "enhance document integrity",
"in today's fast-paced world", "in today's digital landscape",
"cutting-edge", "game-changing", "revolutionize"
BANNED OPENERS (no section may start with): "[website] offers",
"[website] provides", "[website] allows", "[website] enables",
"[website] makes it easy", "[website] helps",
"It is important to note that", "In today's world",
"Many users find that", "This is a great way to",
"As we mentioned", "As mentioned above"
BANNED CLOSERS (no section may end with): "By understanding...",
"By being aware of...", "By mastering...", "Understanding these
differences is essential...", "This ensures that...",
"This is crucial for...", or any sentence that summarizes the
section without adding new information.,
FILLER TEST: Before finalizing each section, apply this test
to every sentence — if you can delete the sentence and the
paragraph still conveys the same information, the sentence is
filler. Remove it and replace with a specific fact, example,
limitation, or actionable detail.
KEYWORD RULE: Embed keywords inside complete sentences only.
Never as list items, headings, or grouped phrases.,
CREDIBILITY: No superlatives ("best", "only", "most powerful")
unless sourced. Name roles specifically (e.g., "a procurement
coordinator at a mid-size manufacturer", not "professionals").
BRAND MENTIONS: The website name from global_config must appear
naturally 5–6 times across the article body. Concentrate in
STEPS, SCENARIOS, COMPARISON, and CLOSING sections. Never more
than 2× in a single paragraph. Never in the opening sentence
of any section.
VOICE: Active voice. Tone follows positioning_statement as
reference — never quote it directly. Write with the confidence
of a product page, not the neutrality of a Wikipedia article.
